parameters.content:
## Copy and paste this sticky with the nodes inside of it into your AI prompt
















_these nodes are just here to give the llm extra context_

## JSON Schema Generator

The nodes below are part of a reusable n8n subworkflow that validates JSON objects against a JSON Schema. I need you to generate a schema for my use case.

The schema gets passed into the `requiredSchema` param and the JSON to validate goes into `paramsToValidate`. Both are wrapped in `={{ }}` so n8n treats them as objects.

### What the Validator Supports

You can use any of these keywords in the schema you generate:

- **Types:** `string`, `number`, `integer`, `boolean`, `array`, `object`, `null`
- **String:** `minLength`, `maxLength`, `pattern` (regex)
- **Number:** `minimum`, `maximum`, `exclusiveMinimum`, `exclusiveMaximum`
- **Value restrictions:** `enum` (list of allowed values), `const` (exact match)
- **Arrays:** `minItems`, `maxItems`, `items` (validates each element recursively)
- **Objects:** `required`, `properties`, `additionalProperties`
- **Conditional logic:** `oneOf`, `anyOf`, `allOf`, `not`
- **`description`** — this is important: the validator pulls descriptions into error messages, so every field needs one

### Rules for Generating the Schema

- Every field MUST have a `description` in plain language — they appear in validation errors
- Use `integer` instead of `number` for whole numbers
- Use `enum` for fields with a fixed set of allowed values
- Use `oneOf` with `const` and `not: { required: [...] }` for conditional required/forbidden fields
- Use `additionalProperties: false` if extra fields should be rejected
- Use `pattern` with regex for formatted strings (emails, URLs, codes, etc.)
- Use `minimum` / `maximum` for numeric bounds
- Use `minLength: 1` for strings that must not be empty
- Use `minItems` for arrays that need at least one entry

### Before Generating

Ask me follow-up questions first to make sure you understand:

- Which fields are required vs optional
- Any conditional logic (e.g. "field X is required only when field Y is a certain value")
- Valid value ranges, enums, or patterns for each field
- Whether unknown/extra fields should be rejected
- Any fields that conflict with each other (e.g. "don't send A and B together")

### Output Format

Ask the user whether they want:

1. **Full n8n nodes** (default) — the complete set of ready-to-paste n8n nodes: the Execute Sub-Workflow node (with the schema already embedded), the If node to check `valid`, and the Respond to Webhook node for returning 400 errors.
2. **Schema only** — just the raw JSON Schema object.

On the **first response**, default to returning the full nodes unless the user says otherwise. On **subsequent changes or revisions**, only return the updated schema — the user already has the nodes and just needs to swap the schema value.

### How to Embed the Schema in n8n

When generating the Execute Sub-Workflow node, the schema must be set correctly in the `requiredSchema` parameter:

- The value must be wrapped in `={{ }}` so n8n evaluates it as a JavaScript expression that returns an object — not a string.
- The schema JSON goes directly between `={{ ` and ` }}` with no quotes around the outer object.
- All regex backslashes inside `pattern` values must be **double-escaped** (`\\\\`) because the expression is evaluated as a JS string first. For example, the regex `^\S+@\S+\.\S+$` becomes `"^\\\\S+@\\\\S+\\\\.\\\\S+$"` in the node parameter.
- The `paramsToValidate` param should be set to `={{ $json.body }}` or wherever the incoming data lives. Remind the user to update this path to match their actual data location.
- The Execute Sub-Workflow node must point to the ID of the Param Schema Validation workflow. Remind the user to re-select the workflow from the dropdown after importing since workflow IDs differ per instance.

### Generating the Full Nodes

When returning full nodes, use the following node structure as a base and only modify the `requiredSchema` value with the generated schema. Keep all other fields, positions, and wiring intact.

The nodes to include are:

1. **Execute Sub-Workflow node** (`n8n-nodes-base.executeWorkflow`) — calls the validator with `requiredSchema` and `paramsToValidate`

2. **If node** (`n8n-nodes-base.if`) — checks `{{ $json.valid }}` is true

3. **Respond to Webhook node** (`n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook`) — returns a 400 with the validation error and schema in the response body

The Respond to Webhook node MUST use this exact response body format:

```

{

  "error": {{ $json.validationError.toJsonString() }},

  "requestSchema": {{ $json.requiredSchema.toJsonString() }}

}

```

Set the response code to `400`. This format ensures the caller gets both the human-readable error string and the full schema they violated, which is especially useful for LLM-based callers that can self-correct.

Include the `connections` object so the nodes are wired: Execute Sub-Workflow → If → (true) continues, (false) → Respond to Webhook with 400.

Do NOT include the Webhook trigger node — the user will already have their own trigger.

### My JSON

The user will provide an example of their JSON request separately. If they do not, ask them for it.

# JSON Schema Validator

## How it works

Call this via an **Execute Sub-Workflow** node with two params:

- **`requiredSchema`** — your JSON Schema (wrapped in `={{ }}`)
- **`paramsToValidate`** — the data to check, e.g. `={{ $json.body }}`

Returns `{ valid: true }` on success. On failure, returns `valid: false` with a `validationError` string and the full `requiredSchema` — useful for error responses or feeding back into an LLM to self-correct.

## Example error output

Validation failed (3 issues):
- name: Missing required field "name" — Customer full name
- email: "not-an-email" is not valid — expected: Contact email address
- plan: "premium" is not an allowed value. Must be one of: starter, pro, enterprise

## Don't know JSON Schema?

Copy the **prompt template sticky note** and its two nodes, paste into any LLM chat with an example of your data, and it'll generate a schema for you.

## Quick start

1. Add an **Execute Sub-Workflow** node pointing to this workflow
2. Set `requiredSchema` to `={{ your_schema_here }}`
3. Set `paramsToValidate` to `={{ $json.body }}`
4. Route on `valid` — `true` continues, `false` handles the error

# Schema Creation
AIs are great at making schemas. Copy and paste the two nodes below with the entire sticky into your agent of choice with an example of your request.


_Out of view in the sticky is a prompt that will help the agent know how to make the schema_

# Usage Example
This is an example showing how to wire the validator into a webhook endpoint

Then the `error` field gives a detailed readable description of all of the errors:

Validation failed (6 issues):
• name: Missing required field \"name\" — Customer full name
• email: \"not-an-email\" is not valid — expected: Contact email address
• plan: \"premium\" is not an allowed value. Must be one of: starter, pro, enterprise — Subscription plan
• seat_count: Expected type \"integer\" but got \"non-integer number\" — Number of licensed seats
• tags: Must have at least 1 item(s), got 0 — At least one tag for categorization
• referral_code: Unknown field \"referral_code\" is not allowed

### The Actual Workflow
These two nodes are the actual functional part of the template, all of the other nodes on the canvas are just for demonstration.

tags.description: At least one tag for categorization

Validate Schema.validationError:
Validation failed (6 issues):
• name: Missing required field "name" — Customer full name
• email: "not-an-email" is not valid — expected: Contact email address
• plan: "premium" is not an allowed value. Must be one of: starter, pro, enterprise — Subscription plan
• seat_count: Expected type "integer" but got "non-integer number" — Number of licensed seats
• tags: Must have at least 1 item(s), got 0 — At least one tag for categorization
• referral_code: Unknown field "referral_code" is not allowed

tags.description: At least one tag for categorization

Return 400 param error.validationError:
Validation failed (6 issues):
• name: Missing required field "name" — Customer full name
• email: "not-an-email" is not valid — expected: Contact email address
• plan: "premium" is not an allowed value. Must be one of: starter, pro, enterprise — Subscription plan
• seat_count: Expected type "integer" but got "non-integer number" — Number of licensed seats
• tags: Must have at least 1 item(s), got 0 — At least one tag for categorization
• referral_code: Unknown field "referral_code" is not allowed
